description: Version of the parent development-instruction resolver contract with explicit invariant and procedural authority layers.
Rollout mode; enforce is active after local-priority, refusal, compatibility, and parent-only CI ownership checks passed in the implementation cycle.
Applicable repository invariant authority; instruction memory is procedural and cannot override this rulebook.
Instruction memory controls development procedure only and never invariant authority.
Only an ENOENT result at the configured target-local path may activate the parent fallback.
Parent fallback document resolved inside the lesson repository.
Canonical target-local instruction document; an existing valid file has procedural priority over the parent fallback.
Data-driven A-F stage mapping and continuation policy.
Existing seven-phase repository workflow authority referenced by the A-F overlay.
Context kind and menu-number authority used instead of fixed menu branches.
Menu-id and target-scope authority joined to the context map by menu number.
Selected product repository registry authority.
Selected product repository per development context.
Target-local operation-mode authority required before parent fallback may govern a product repository.
Target-local invariant entry required before a product instruction source is resolved.
Product Git-mode policy used to calculate the maximum applicable D stage.
Saved product Git-mode setting used before policy defaults.
Action-policy defaults and allowed values for normal Git/GitHub work.
Saved Git/GitHub action settings intersected with task scope and product mode.
Workflow kinds eligible for the parent-repository fallback.
Workflow kinds eligible for selected product-repository fallback.
Menu profile scope required for the parent repository target.
Menu profile scope required for product repository targets.
Repo-local workflow skill selected for lesson-repository development.
Product-local workflow skill selected for external product development.
Only a ready parent-managed product repository may use the parent fallback.
Companion operation-mode flag required for parent-managed fallback.
Existing versionless local documents use the compatibility profile; declared unknown versions fail closed.
Strict version required from the parent fallback document.
Required unique second-level workflow headings.
Strict anchors required only from the parent fallback profile.
Maximum bounded instruction document size.
Maximum safe task-scope identifier length accepted as runtime authorization evidence.
Maximum Git/CI applicability for the lesson repository before action settings narrow it.

Additive Next Workflow tracked activation baseline and schema source; planned and shadow modes grant no authority, and enforced Production authority is accepted only from the protected external-trust-backed store.

Release prerequisite authority that requires accepted headless runtime, one immutable candidate, complete exact-range parent document synchronization, signed release and transition evidence, and enforced activation; paused Control Center reconstruction neither grants nor blocks headless authority.
Machine-readable local-first instruction and parent-management composition contract for parent-managed child repositories.

Machine-readable Orchestrator, Lead Agent, Task Agent, delegation, rigor, ownership, protected runtime, exact provenance, and independent review contract; CI-only non-executed provider fixtures used by bootstrap, launcher-integrity, or injected discovery tests are test data and cannot satisfy this authority, while missing real containment skips both positive suites and requires guided refusal rather than simulation.
